svg:svg.svg:text: 4 key molecular descriptors explain anion binding: num_O, TPSA, HBA, HOMO

svg:g.svg:text: num_O
↑ Positive
More oxygen atoms → more coordination sites with Pb²⁺
Oxygen-rich anions form stronger bonds with perovskite surface lead atoms
LR coefficient: positive direction → higher Eb
TPSA
↑ Positive
Larger polar surface area → more hydrogen bonding interactions
Topological Polar Surface Area measures hydrogen bonding capacity
LR coefficient: positive direction → higher Eb
HBA
↑ Positive
More H-bond acceptors → stronger interaction with surface
Hydrogen Bond Acceptors directly relate to surface binding strength
LR coefficient: positive direction → higher Eb
HOMO
↓ Negative
Lower HOMO → higher electronegativity → stronger Eb
Highest Occupied Molecular Orbital energy from Gaussian calculations
LR coefficient: negative direction → lower HOMO = higher Eb
Logistic Regression Coefficients (Round 1)

svg:svg.svg:text: • Anions with more O atoms bind more strongly to Pb²⁺ surface
• Larger polar surface area enables stronger hydrogen bonding
• More H-bond acceptors provide additional interaction pathways
• Lower HOMO = higher electronegativity → stronger electron donation
Source: RF importance + LR coefficients from 17-feature Round 1 training
